zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Harrope於1603年出生在英國史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，她的父親Sir Richard Harrope21歲，母親Lady Joanne Phillippes25歲。她於1627年1月7日與Sir John Williamson在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1627年住在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福。她去世於1701年在法國皮卡迪享年98歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Harrope于1603年出生在英国史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特时，她的父亲Sir Richard Harrope21岁，母亲Lady Joanne Phillippes25岁。她于1627年1月7日与Sir John Williamson在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1627年住在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福。她去世于1701年在法国皮卡迪享年98岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Harrop, Harrop Dale, Harrop Hall (Yorkshire), or Harehope (Northumberland), all of which may derive from Old English hara ‘hare’ + hop ‘small enclosed valley’.

variants.name: Harrup
meanings: Probably an altered form of German   Harruff  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: Reigns of Louis XIII and Louis XIV. Royal power at its peak. France dominates Europe, French culture spreads. Start of large-scale sea trade.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: The Palace of Versailles
historicEvents.description: The Palace of Versailles was originally known as a hunting log and retreat for Louis XII. When Louis XIV took over he moved his family into it. After he died the palace was abandoned. It has since been turned into a museum.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Harrope was born in 1603, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, her father, Sir Richard Harrope, was 21 and her mother, Lady Joanne Phillippes, was 25. She married Sir John Williamson on 7 January 1627, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She lived in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England in 1627. She died in 1701, in Picardie, France, at the age of 98.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Harrope est née en 1603, à Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Harrope, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lady Joanne Phillippes, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Williamson le 7 janvier 1627, à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Elle a vécu à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1627. Elle est décédée en 1701, à Picardie, France, à l’âge de 98 ans.

Januar 1627 in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England Sir John Williamson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1627 hat sie in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England gelebt. Sie starb 1701 im Alter von 98 Jahren in Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Harrope è nata il nel 1603 nel Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Sir Richard Harrope, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Lady Joanne Phillippes, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Williamson il 7 gennaio 1627, nel Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Vissuto a Wilmslow, Cheshire, England nel 1627. Morì nel 1701, nel Picardie, Francia, all'età di 98 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Harropeが1603年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアモーエトン コルベットで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Harropeは21歳，母Lady Joanne Phillippesは25歳であった。1627年1月7日にイギリスチェシュアイアウィルムスロウでSir John Williamsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1627年にはイギリスチェシュアイアウィルムスロウに住んでいた。彼女は1701年にフランスピカーディで98歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Harrope(이)가 1603년 영국 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard Harrope(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joanne Phillippes(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1627년 1월 7일 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 Sir John Williamson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1627년도에 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 살았다. 그녀는 1701년 프랑스 피카디에서 98세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Harrope nasceu em 1603, em Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Harrope, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joanne Phillippes, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Williamson em 7 de janeiro de 1627, em Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1627. Ela faleceu em 1701, em Picardie, França, com 98 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Harrope родилась в 1603 г. в местности моретон корбет, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard Harrope было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Joanne Phillippes, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Williamson 7 января 1627 г. в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия в 1627 году. Она умерла в 1701 г. в местности пикарди, Франция в возрасте 98 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Harrope nació en 1603, en Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Harrope, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Lady Joanne Phillippes, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir John Williamson el 7 de enero de 1627, en Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Vivió en Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1627. Murió en 1701, en Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 98 años.
